RDFLib
======
[![Build Status](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/actions/workflows/validate.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/actions?query=branch%3Amain)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/rdflib/badge/?version=latest)](https://rdflib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
[![Coveralls branch](https://img.shields.io/coveralls/RDFLib/rdflib/main.svg)](https://coveralls.io/r/RDFLib/rdflib?branch=main)

[![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/RDFLib/rdflib.svg)](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/stargazers)
[![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/rdflib/week)](https://pepy.tech/project/rdflib)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/rdflib.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rdflib)

[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.6845245.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6845245)

[![Contribute with Gitpod](https://img.shields.io/badge/Contribute%20with-Gitpod-908a85?logo=gitpod)](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib)
[![Gitter](https://badges.gitter.im/RDFLib/rdflib.svg)](https://gitter.im/RDFLib/rdflib?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=pr-badge)
[![Matrix](https://img.shields.io/matrix/rdflib:matrix.org?label=matrix.org%20chat)](https://matrix.to/#/#RDFLib_rdflib:gitter.im)

RDFLib is a pure Python package for working with [RDF](http://www.w3.org/RDF/). RDFLib contains most things you need to work with RDF, including:

* parsers and serializers for RDF/XML, N3, NTriples, N-Quads, Turtle, TriX, Trig and JSON-LD
* a Graph interface which can be backed by any one of a number of Store implementations
* store implementations for in-memory, persistent on disk (Berkeley DB) and remote SPARQL endpoints
* a SPARQL 1.1 implementation - supporting SPARQL 1.1 Queries and Update statements
* SPARQL function extension mechanisms

## RDFlib Family of packages
The RDFlib community maintains many RDF-related Python code repositories with different purposes. For example:

* [rdflib](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib) - the RDFLib core
* [sparqlwrapper](https://github.com/RDFLib/sparqlwrapper) - a simple Python wrapper around a SPARQL service to remotely execute your queries
* [pyLODE](https://github.com/RDFLib/pyLODE) - An OWL ontology documentation tool using Python and templating, based on LODE.
* [pyrdfa3](https://github.com/RDFLib/pyrdfa3) - RDFa 1.1 distiller/parser library: can extract RDFa 1.1/1.0 from (X)HTML, SVG, or XML in general.
* [pymicrodata](https://github.com/RDFLib/pymicrodata) - A module to extract RDF from an HTML5 page annotated with microdata. 
* [pySHACL](https://github.com/RDFLib/pySHACL) - A pure Python module which allows for the validation of RDF graphs against SHACL graphs.
* [OWL-RL](https://github.com/RDFLib/OWL-RL) - A simple implementation of the OWL2 RL Profile which expands the graph with all possible triples that OWL RL defines.

Please see the list for all packages/repositories here:

* <https://github.com/RDFLib>

Help with maintenance of all of the RDFLib family of packages is always welcome and appreciated.

## Versions & Releases

* `main` branch in this repository is the unstable release
* `7.1.1` current stable release, bugfixes to 7.1.0
* `7.0.0` previous stable release, supports Python 3.8.1+ only.
    * see [Releases](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/releases)
* `6.x.y` supports Python 3.7+ only. Many improvements over 5.0.0
    * see [Releases](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/releases)
* `5.x.y` supports Python 2.7 and 3.4+ and is [mostly backwards compatible with 4.2.2](https://rdflib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/upgrade4to5.html).

See <https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/releases/> for the release details.

## Documentation
See <https://rdflib.readthedocs.io> for our documentation built from the code. Note that there are `latest`, `stable` and versioned builds, such as `5.0.0`, matching releases.

## Installation
The stable release of RDFLib may be installed with Python's package management tool *pip*:

    $ pip install rdflib

Some features of RDFLib require optional dependencies which may be installed using *pip* extras:

    $ pip install rdflib[berkeleydb,networkx,html,lxml,orjson]

Alternatively manually download the package from the Python Package
Index (PyPI) at https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rdflib

The current version of RDFLib is 7.1.1, see the ``CHANGELOG.md`` file for what's new in this release.

### Installation of the current main branch (for developers)

With *pip* you can also install rdflib from the git repository with one of the following options:

    $ pip install git+https://github.com/rdflib/rdflib@main

or

    $ pip install -e git+https://github.com/rdflib/rdflib@main#egg=rdflib

or from your locally cloned repository you can install it with one of the following options:

    $ poetry install  # installs into a poetry-managed venv

or

    $ pip install -e .

## Getting Started
RDFLib aims to be a pythonic RDF API. RDFLib's main data object is a `Graph` which is a Python collection
of RDF *Subject, Predicate, Object* Triples:

To create graph and load it with RDF data from DBPedia then print the results:

```python
from rdflib import Graph
g = Graph()
g.parse('http://dbpedia.org/resource/Semantic_Web')

for s, p, o in g:
    print(s, p, o)
```
The components of the triples are URIs (resources) or Literals
(values).

URIs are grouped together by *namespace*, common namespaces are included in RDFLib:

```python
from rdflib.namespace import DC, DCTERMS, DOAP, FOAF, SKOS, OWL, RDF, RDFS, VOID, XMLNS, XSD
```

You can use them like this:

```python
from rdflib import Graph, URIRef, Literal
from rdflib.namespace import RDFS, XSD

g = Graph()
semweb = URIRef('http://dbpedia.org/resource/Semantic_Web')
type = g.value(semweb, RDFS.label)
```
Where `RDFS` is the RDFS namespace, `XSD` the XML Schema Datatypes namespace and `g.value` returns an object of the triple-pattern given (or an arbitrary one if multiple exist).

Or like this, adding a triple to a graph `g`:

```python
g.add((
    URIRef("http://example.com/person/nick"),
    FOAF.givenName,
    Literal("Nick", datatype=XSD.string)
))
```
The triple (in n-triples notation) `<http://example.com/person/nick> <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/givenName> "Nick"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string> .`
is created where the property `FOAF.givenName` is the URI `<http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/givenName>` and `XSD.string` is the
URI `<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>`.

You can bind namespaces to prefixes to shorten the URIs for RDF/XML, Turtle, N3, TriG, TriX & JSON-LD serializations:

 ```python
g.bind("foaf", FOAF)
g.bind("xsd", XSD)
```
This will allow the n-triples triple above to be serialised like this:
 ```python
print(g.serialize(format="turtle"))
```

With these results:
```turtle
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>

<http://example.com/person/nick> foaf:givenName "Nick"^^xsd:string .
```

New Namespaces can also be defined:

```python
dbpedia = Namespace('http://dbpedia.org/ontology/')

abstracts = list(x for x in g.objects(semweb, dbpedia['abstract']) if x.language=='en')
```

See also [./examples](./examples)


## Features
The library contains parsers and serializers for RDF/XML, N3,
NTriples, N-Quads, Turtle, TriX, JSON-LD, RDFa and Microdata.

The library presents a Graph interface which can be backed by
any one of a number of Store implementations.

This core RDFLib package includes store implementations for
in-memory storage and persistent storage on top of the Berkeley DB.

A SPARQL 1.1 implementation is included - supporting SPARQL 1.1 Queries and Update statements.

RDFLib is open source and is maintained on [GitHub](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/). RDFLib releases, current and previous
are listed on [PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rdflib/)

Multiple other projects are contained within the RDFlib "family", see <https://github.com/RDFLib/>.

## Running tests

### Running the tests on the host

Run the test suite with `pytest`.
```shell
poetry install
poetry run pytest
```

### Running test coverage on the host with coverage report

Run the test suite and generate a HTML coverage report with `pytest` and `pytest-cov`.
```shell
poetry run pytest --cov
```

### Viewing test coverage

Once tests have produced HTML output of the coverage report, view it by running:
```shell
poetry run pytest --cov --cov-report term --cov-report html
python -m http.server --directory=htmlcov
```

## Contributing

RDFLib survives and grows via user contributions!
Please read our [contributing guide](https://rdflib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/CONTRIBUTING.html) and [developers guide](https://rdflib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/developers.html) to get started.
Please consider lodging Pull Requests here:

* <https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/pulls>

To get a development environment consider using Gitpod or Google Cloud Shell.

[![Open in Gitpod](https://gitpod.io/button/open-in-gitpod.svg)](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib)

You can also raise issues here:

* <https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/issues>

## Support & Contacts
For general "how do I..." queries, please use https://stackoverflow.com and tag your question with `rdflib`.
Existing questions:

* <https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/rdflib>

If you want to contact the rdflib maintainers, please do so via:

* the rdflib-dev mailing list: <https://groups.google.com/group/rdflib-dev>

modules.name: rdflib.events
documentation:
Dirt Simple Events

A Dispatcher (or a subclass of Dispatcher) stores event handlers that
are 'fired' simple event objects when interesting things happen.

Create a dispatcher:

  >>> d = Dispatcher()

Now create a handler for the event and subscribe it to the dispatcher
to handle Event events.  A handler is a simple function or method that
accepts the event as an argument:

  >>> def handler1(event): print(repr(event))
  >>> d.subscribe(Event, handler1) # doctest: +ELLIPSIS
  <rdflib.events.Dispatcher object at ...>

Now dispatch a new event into the dispatcher, and see handler1 get
fired:

  >>> d.dispatch(Event(foo='bar', data='yours', used_by='the event handlers'))
  <rdflib.events.Event ['data', 'foo', 'used_by']>

classes.name: rdflib.events.Event
documentation:
An event is a container for attributes.  The source of an event
creates this object, or a subclass, gives it any kind of data that
the events handlers need to handle the event, and then calls
notify(event).

The target of an event registers a function to handle the event it
is interested with subscribe().  When a sources calls
notify(event), each subscriber to that event will be called in no
particular order.

classes.name: rdflib.events.Dispatcher

methods.name: rdflib.events.Dispatcher.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribe the given handler to an event_type.  Handlers
are called in the order they are subscribed.

methods.name: rdflib.events.Dispatcher.dispatch
documentation:
Dispatch the given event to the subscribed handlers for
the event's type

An object that can dispatch events to a privately managed group of
subscribers.

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.classPartition
documentation: A subset of a void:Dataset that contains only the entities of a certain rdfs:Class.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.classes
documentation: The total number of distinct classes in a void:Dataset. In other words, the number of distinct resources occurring as objects of rdf:type triples in the dataset.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.dataDump
documentation: An RDF dump, partial or complete, of a void:Dataset.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.distinctObjects
documentation: The total number of distinct objects in a void:Dataset. In other words, the number of distinct resources that occur in the object position of triples in the dataset. Literals are included in this count.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.distinctSubjects
documentation: The total number of distinct subjects in a void:Dataset. In other words, the number of distinct resources that occur in the subject position of triples in the dataset.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.documents
documentation: The total number of documents, for datasets that are published as a set of individual documents, such as RDF/XML documents or RDFa-annotated web pages. Non-RDF documents, such as web pages in HTML or images, are usually not included in this count. This property is intended for datasets where the total number of triples or entities is hard to determine. void:triples or void:entities should be preferred where practical.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.exampleResource
documentation: example resource of dataset

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.openSearchDescription

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.properties

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.property
documentation: The rdf:Property that is the predicate of all triples in a property-based partition.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.propertyPartition

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.rootResource
documentation: A top concept or entry point for a void:Dataset that is structured in a tree-like fashion. All resources in a dataset can be reached by following links from its root resources in a small number of steps.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.sparqlEndpoint

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.subset

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.target

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.triples

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.uriSpace

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.vocabulary

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.Dataset
documentation: A set of RDF triples that are published, maintained or aggregated by a single provider.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.DatasetDescription
documentation: A web resource whose foaf:primaryTopic or foaf:topics include void:Datasets.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.Linkset
documentation: A collection of RDF links between two void:Datasets.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._VOID.VOID.TechnicalFeature
documentation: A technical feature of a void:Dataset, such as a supported RDF serialization format.

Vocabulary of Interlinked Datasets (VoID) The Vocabulary of Interlinked Datasets (VoID) is an RDF Schema vocabulary for expressing metadata about RDF datasets. It is intended as a bridge between the publishers and users of RDF data, with applications ranging from data discovery to cataloging and archiving of datasets. This document provides a formal definition of the new RDF classes and properties introduced for VoID. It is a companion to the main specification document for VoID, Describing Linked Datasets with the VoID Vocabulary. Generated from: http://rdfs.org/ns/void# Date: 2020-05-26 14:20:11.911298

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.ActuatableProperty
documentation: An actuatable quality (property, characteristic) of a FeatureOfInterest.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Actuation
documentation: An Actuation carries out an (Actuation) Procedure to change the state of the world using an Actuator.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Actuator
documentation: A device that is used by, or implements, an (Actuation) Procedure that changes the state of the world.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.FeatureOfInterest
documentation: The thing whose property is being estimated or calculated in the course of an Observation to arrive at a Result or whose property is being manipulated by an Actuator, or which is being sampled or transformed in an act of Sampling.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.ObservableProperty
documentation: An observable quality (property, characteristic) of a FeatureOfInterest.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Observation
documentation: Act of carrying out an (Observation) Procedure to estimate or calculate a value of a property of a FeatureOfInterest. Links to a Sensor to describe what made the Observation and how; links to an ObservableProperty to describe what the result is an estimate of, and to a FeatureOfInterest to detail what that property was associated with.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.ObservationCollection
documentation: Collection of one or more observations, whose members share a common value for one or more property

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Platform
documentation: A Platform is an entity that hosts other entities, particularly Sensors, Actuators, Samplers, and other Platforms.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Procedure
documentation: A workflow, protocol, plan, algorithm, or computational method specifying how to make an Observation, create a Sample, or make a change to the state of the world (via an Actuator). A Procedure is re-usable, and might be involved in many Observations, Samplings, or Actuations. It explains the steps to be carried out to arrive at reproducible results.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Result
documentation: The Result of an Observation, Actuation, or act of Sampling. To store an observation's simple result value one can use the hasSimpleResult property.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Sample
documentation: Feature which is intended to be representative of a FeatureOfInterest on which Observations may be made.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Sampler
documentation: A device that is used by, or implements, a Sampling Procedure to create or transform one or more samples.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Sampling
documentation: An act of Sampling carries out a sampling Procedure to create or transform one or more samples.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.Sensor
documentation: Device, agent (including humans), or software (simulation) involved in, or implementing, a Procedure. Sensors respond to a stimulus, e.g., a change in the environment, or input data composed from the results of prior Observations, and generate a Result. Sensors can be hosted by Platforms.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.resultTime
documentation: The result time is the instant of time when the Observation, Actuation or Sampling activity was completed.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.actsOnProperty
documentation: Relation between an Actuation and the property of a FeatureOfInterest it is acting upon.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasFeatureOfInterest
documentation: A relation between an Observation and the entity whose quality was observed, or between an Actuation and the entity whose property was modified, or between an act of Sampling and the entity that was sampled.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasMember
documentation: Link to a member of a collection of observations that share the same value for one or more of the characteristic properties

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasOriginalSample
documentation: link to the original sample that is related to the context sample through a chain of isSampleOf relations

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasResult
documentation: Relation linking an Observation or Actuation or act of Sampling and a Result or Sample.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasSample
documentation: Relation between a FeatureOfInterest and the Sample used to represent it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasSampledFeature
documentation: link to the ultimate feature of interest of the context sample - i.e. the end of a chain of isSampleOf relations

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hasUltimateFeatureOfInterest
documentation: link to the ultimate feature of interest of an observation or act of sampling. This is useful when the proximate feature of interest is a sample of the ultimate feature of interest, directly or trasntitively.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.hosts
documentation: Relation between a Platform and a Sensor, Actuator, Sampler, or Platform, hosted or mounted on it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isActedOnBy
documentation: Relation between an ActuatableProperty of a FeatureOfInterest and an Actuation changing its state.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isFeatureOfInterestOf
documentation: A relation between a FeatureOfInterest and an Observation about it, an Actuation acting on it, or an act of Sampling that sampled it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isHostedBy
documentation: Relation between a Sensor, Actuator, Sampler, or Platform, and the Platform that it is mounted on or hosted by.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isObservedBy
documentation: Relation between an ObservableProperty and the Sensor able to observe it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isResultOf
documentation: Relation linking a Result to the Observation or Actuation or act of Sampling that created or caused it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.isSampleOf
documentation: Relation from a Sample to the FeatureOfInterest that it is intended to be representative of.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.madeActuation
documentation: Relation between an Actuator and the Actuation it has made.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.madeByActuator
documentation: Relation linking an Actuation to the Actuator that made that Actuation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.madeBySampler
documentation: Relation linking an act of Sampling to the Sampler (sampling device or entity) that made it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.madeBySensor
documentation: Relation between an Observation and the Sensor which made the Observation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.madeSampling
documentation: Relation between a Sampler (sampling device or entity) and the Sampling act it performed.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.observedProperty
documentation: Relation linking an Observation to the property that was observed. The ObservableProperty should be a property of the FeatureOfInterest (linked by hasFeatureOfInterest) of this Observation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.observes
documentation: Relation between a Sensor and an ObservableProperty that it is capable of sensing.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.phenomenonTime
documentation: The time that the Result of an Observation, Actuation or Sampling applies to the FeatureOfInterest. Not necessarily the same as the resultTime. May be an Interval or an Instant, or some other compound TemporalEntity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SOSA.SOSA.usedProcedure
documentation: A relation to link to a re-usable Procedure used in making an Observation, an Actuation, or a Sample, typically through a Sensor, Actuator or Sampler.

Sensor, Observation, Sample, and Actuator (SOSA) Ontology

This ontology is based on the SSN Ontology by the W3C Semantic Sensor Networks Incubator Group (SSN-XG),
together with considerations from the W3C/OGC Spatial Data on the Web Working Group.

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.generalDay
documentation: Day of month - formulated as a text string with a pattern constraint to reproduce the same lexical form as gDay, except that values up to 99 are permitted, in order to support calendars with more than 31 days in a month.  Note that the value-space is not defined, so a generic OWL2 processor cannot compute ordering relationships of values of this type.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.generalMonth
documentation: Month of year - formulated as a text string with a pattern constraint to reproduce the same lexical form as gMonth, except that values up to 20 are permitted, in order to support calendars with more than 12 months in the year.  Note that the value-space is not defined, so a generic OWL2 processor cannot compute ordering relationships of values of this type.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.generalYear
documentation: Year number - formulated as a text string with a pattern constraint to reproduce the same lexical form as gYear, but not restricted to values from the Gregorian calendar.  Note that the value-space is not defined, so a generic OWL2 processor cannot compute ordering relationships of values of this type.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.DateTimeDescription
documentation: Description of date and time structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system. The temporal reference system is fixed to Gregorian Calendar, and the range of year, month, day properties restricted to corresponding XML Schema types xsd:gYear, xsd:gMonth and xsd:gDay, respectively.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.DateTimeInterval
documentation: DateTimeInterval is a subclass of ProperInterval, defined using the multi-element DateTimeDescription.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.DayOfWeek
documentation: The day of week

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.Duration
documentation: Duration of a temporal extent expressed as a number scaled by a temporal unit

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.DurationDescription
documentation: Description of temporal extent structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system. The temporal reference system is fixed to Gregorian Calendar, and the range of each of the numeric properties is restricted to xsd:decimal

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.GeneralDateTimeDescription
documentation: Description of date and time structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.GeneralDurationDescription
documentation: Description of temporal extent structured with separate values for the various elements of a calendar-clock system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.Instant
documentation: A temporal entity with zero extent or duration

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.Interval
documentation: A temporal entity with an extent or duration

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.MonthOfYear
documentation: The month of the year

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.ProperInterval
documentation: A temporal entity with non-zero extent or duration, i.e. for which the value of the beginning and end are different

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TRS
documentation: A temporal reference system, such as a temporal coordinate system (with an origin, direction, and scale), a calendar-clock combination, or a (possibly hierarchical) ordinal system.   This is a stub class, representing the set of all temporal reference systems.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TemporalDuration
documentation: Time extent; duration of a time interval separate from its particular start position

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TemporalEntity
documentation: A temporal interval or instant.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TemporalPosition
documentation: A position on a time-line

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TemporalUnit
documentation: A standard duration, which provides a scale factor for a time extent, or the granularity or precision for a time position.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TimePosition
documentation: A temporal position described using either a (nominal) value from an ordinal reference system, or a (numeric) value in a temporal coordinate system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.TimeZone
documentation: A Time Zone specifies the amount by which the local time is offset from UTC.  	A time zone is usually denoted geographically (e.g. Australian Eastern Daylight Time), with a constant value in a given region.  The region where it applies and the offset from UTC are specified by a locally recognised governing authority.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.day
documentation: Day position in a calendar-clock system.  The range of this property is not specified, so can be replaced by any specific representation of a calendar day from any calendar.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.dayOfYear
documentation: The number of the day within the year

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.days
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in days

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasXSDDuration
documentation: Extent of a temporal entity, expressed using xsd:duration

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hour
documentation: Hour position in a calendar-clock system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hours
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in hours

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inXSDDate
documentation: Position of an instant, expressed using xsd:date

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inXSDgYear
documentation: Position of an instant, expressed using xsd:gYear

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inXSDgYearMonth
documentation: Position of an instant, expressed using xsd:gYearMonth

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.minute
documentation: Minute position in a calendar-clock system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.minutes
documentation: length, or element of, a temporal extent expressed in minutes

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.month
documentation: Month position in a calendar-clock system.  The range of this property is not specified, so can be replaced by any specific representation of a calendar month from any calendar.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.months
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in months

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.nominalPosition
documentation: The (nominal) value indicating temporal position in an ordinal reference system

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.numericDuration
documentation: Value of a temporal extent expressed as a decimal number scaled by a temporal unit

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.numericPosition
documentation: The (numeric) value indicating position within a temporal coordinate system

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.second
documentation: Second position in a calendar-clock system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.seconds
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in seconds

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.week
documentation: Week number within the year.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.weeks
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in weeks

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.year
documentation: Year position in a calendar-clock system.  The range of this property is not specified, so can be replaced by any specific representation of a calendar year from any calendar.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.years
documentation: length of, or element of the length of, a temporal extent expressed in years

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.January
documentation: January

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.Year
documentation: Year duration

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inXSDDateTime
documentation: Position of an instant, expressed using xsd:dateTime

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.xsdDateTime
documentation: Value of DateTimeInterval expressed as a compact value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasTRS
documentation: The temporal reference system used by a temporal position or extent description.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.after
documentation: Gives directionality to time. If a temporal entity T1 is after another temporal entity T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasBeginning
documentation: Beginning of a temporal entity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasDateTimeDescription
documentation: Value of DateTimeInterval expressed as a structured value. The beginning and end of the interval coincide with the limits of the shortest element in the description.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasDuration
documentation: Duration of a temporal entity, event or activity, or thing, expressed as a scaled value

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasDurationDescription
documentation: Duration of a temporal entity, expressed using a structured description

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasEnd
documentation: End of a temporal entity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasTemporalDuration
documentation: Duration of a temporal entity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.hasTime
documentation: Supports the association of a temporal entity (instant or interval) to any thing

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inTemporalPosition
documentation: Position of a time instant

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inTimePosition
documentation: Position of a time instant expressed as a TimePosition

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.inside
documentation: An instant that falls inside the interval. It is not intended to include beginnings and ends of intervals.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalAfter
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalAfter another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalBefore
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalBefore another proper interval T2, then the end of T1 is before the beginning of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalContains
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalContains another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is before the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is after the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalDisjoint
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalDisjoint another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the end of T2, or the end of T1 is before the beginning of T2, i.e. the intervals do not overlap in any way, but their ordering relationship is not known.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalDuring
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalDuring another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is before the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalEquals
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalEquals another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is coincident with the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is coincident with the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalFinishedBy
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalFinishedBy another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is before the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is coincident with the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalFinishes
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalFinishes another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is coincident with the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalIn
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalIn another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the beginning of T2 or is coincident with the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is before the end of T2, or is coincident with the end of T2, except that end of T1 may not be coincident with the end of T2 if the beginning of T1 is coincident with the beginning of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalMeets
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalMeets another proper interval T2, then the end of T1 is coincident with the beginning of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalMetBy
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalMetBy another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is coincident with the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalOverlappedBy
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalOverlappedBy another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is after the beginning of T2, the beginning of T1 is before the end of T2, and the end of T1 is after the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalOverlaps
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalOverlaps another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is before the beginning of T2, the end of T1 is after the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is before the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalStartedBy
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalStarted another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is coincident with the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is after the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.intervalStarts
documentation: If a proper interval T1 is intervalStarts another proper interval T2, then the beginning of T1 is coincident with the beginning of T2, and the end of T1 is before the end of T2.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.monthOfYear
documentation: The month of the year, whose value is a member of the class time:MonthOfYear

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.timeZone
documentation: The time zone for clock elements in the temporal position

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.unitType
documentation: The temporal unit which provides the precision of a date-time value or scale of a temporal extent

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._TIME.TIME.before
documentation: Gives directionality to time. If a temporal entity T1 is before another temporal entity T2, then the end of T1 is before the beginning of T2. Thus, "before" can be considered to be basic to instants and derived for intervals.

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.aboutUrl
documentation: A URI template property that MAY be used to indicate what a cell contains information about.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.base
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single string: a term defined in the default context representing a built-in datatype URL, as listed above.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.column
documentation: An array property of column descriptions as described in section 5.6 Columns.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.columnReference
documentation: A column reference property that holds either a single reference to a column description object within this schema, or an array of references. These form the referencing columns for the foreign key definition.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.commentPrefix
documentation: An atomic property that sets the comment prefix flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.datatype
documentation: An object property that contains either a single string that is the main datatype of the values of the cell or a datatype description object. If the value of this property is a string, it MUST be one of the built-in datatypes defined in section 5.11.1 Built-in Datatypes or an absolute URL; if it is an object then it describes a more specialised datatype.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.decimalChar
documentation: A string whose value is used to represent a decimal point within the number.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.default
documentation: An atomic property holding a single string that is used to create a default value for the cell in cases where the original string value is an empty string.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.delimiter
documentation: An atomic property that sets the delimiter flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.describes
documentation: From IANA describes: The relationship A 'describes' B asserts that resource A provides a description of resource B. There are no constraints on the format or representation of either A or B, neither are there any further constraints on either resource.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.dialect
documentation: An object property that provides a single dialect description. If provided, dialect provides hints to processors about how to parse the referenced files to create tabular data models for the tables in the group.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.doubleQuote
documentation: A boolean atomic property that, if `true`, sets the escape character flag to `"`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.encoding
documentation: An atomic property that sets the encoding flag to the single provided string value, which MUST be a defined in [[encoding]]. The default is "utf-8".

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.foreignKey
documentation: For a Table: a list of foreign keys on the table.  For a Schema: an array property of foreign key definitions that define how the values from specified columns within this table link to rows within this table or other tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.format
documentation: An atomic property that contains either a single string or an object that defines the format of a value of this type, used when parsing a string value as described in Parsing Cells in [[tabular-data-model]].

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.groupChar
documentation: A string whose value is used to group digits within the number.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.header
documentation: A boolean atomic property that, if `true`, sets the header row count flag to `1`, and if `false` to `0`, unless headerRowCount is provided, in which case the value provided for the header property is ignored.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.headerRowCount
documentation: An numeric atomic property that sets the header row count flag to the single provided value, which must be a non-negative integer.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.lang
documentation: An atomic property giving a single string language code as defined by [[BCP47]].

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.length
documentation: The exact length of the value of the cell.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.lineTerminators
documentation: An atomic property that sets the line terminators flag to either an array containing the single provided string value, or the provided array.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.maxExclusive
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single number that is the maximum valid value (exclusive).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.maxInclusive
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single number that is the maximum valid value (inclusive).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.maxLength
documentation: A numeric atomic property that contains a single integer that is the maximum length of the value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.minExclusive
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single number that is the minimum valid value (exclusive).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.minInclusive
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single number that is the minimum valid value (inclusive).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.minLength
documentation: An atomic property that contains a single integer that is the minimum length of the value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.name
documentation: An atomic property that gives a single canonical name for the column. The value of this property becomes the name annotation for the described column.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.note
documentation: An array property that provides an array of objects representing arbitrary annotations on the annotated tabular data model.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.null
documentation: An atomic property giving the string or strings used for null values within the data. If the string value of the cell is equal to any one of these values, the cell value is `null`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.ordered
documentation: A boolean atomic property taking a single value which indicates whether a list that is the value of the cell is ordered (if `true`) or unordered (if `false`).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.pattern
documentation: A regular expression string, in the syntax and interpreted as defined by [[ECMASCRIPT]].

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.primaryKey
documentation: For Schema: A column reference property that holds either a single reference to a column description object or an array of references.  For Row: a possibly empty list of cells whose values together provide a unique identifier for this row. This is similar to the name of a column.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.propertyUrl
documentation: An URI template property that MAY be used to create a URI for a property if the table is mapped to another format.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.quoteChar
documentation: An atomic property that sets the quote character flag to the single provided value, which must be a string or `null`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.reference
documentation: An object property that identifies a **referenced table** and a set of **referenced columns** within that table.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.referencedRow
documentation: A possibly empty list of pairs of a foreign key and a row in a table within the same group of tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.required
documentation: A boolean atomic property taking a single value which indicates whether the cell must have a non-null value. The default is `false`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.resource
documentation: A link property holding a URL that is the identifier for a specific table that is being referenced.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.row
documentation: Relates a Table to each Row output.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.rowTitle
documentation: A column reference property that holds either a single reference to a column description object or an array of references.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.rownum
documentation: The position of the row amongst the rows of the Annotated Tabl, starting from 1

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.schemaReference
documentation: A link property holding a URL that is the identifier for a schema that is being referenced.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.scriptFormat
documentation: A link property giving the single URL for the format that is used by the script or template.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.separator
documentation: An atomic property that MUST have a single string value that is the character used to separate items in the string value of the cell.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.skipBlankRows
documentation: An boolean atomic property that sets the `skip blank rows` flag to the single provided boolean value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.skipColumns
documentation: An numeric atomic property that sets the `skip columns` flag to the single provided numeric value, which MUST be a non-negative integer.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.skipInitialSpace
documentation: A boolean atomic property that, if `true`, sets the trim flag to "start". If `false`, to `false`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.skipRows
documentation: An numeric atomic property that sets the `skip rows` flag to the single provided numeric value, which MUST be a non-negative integer.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.source
documentation: A single string atomic property that provides, if specified, the format to which the tabular data should be transformed prior to the transformation using the script or template.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.suppressOutput
documentation: A boolean atomic property. If `true`, suppresses any output that would be generated when converting a table or cells within a column.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.table
documentation: Relates an Table group to annotated tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.tableDirection
documentation: One of `rtl`, `ltr` or `auto`. Indicates whether the tables in the group should be displayed with the first column on the right, on the left, or based on the first character in the table that has a specific direction.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.tableSchema
documentation: An object property that provides a single schema description as described in section 5.5 Schemas, used as the default for all the tables in the group

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.targetFormat
documentation: A link property giving the single URL for the format that will be created through the transformation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.textDirection
documentation: An atomic property that must have a single value that is one of `rtl` or `ltr` (the default).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.title
documentation: For a Transformation A natural language property that describes the format that will be generated from the transformation.  For a Column: A natural language property that provides possible alternative names for the column.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.transformations
documentation: An array property of transformation definitions that provide mechanisms to transform the tabular data into other formats.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.trim
documentation: An atomic property that, if the boolean `true`, sets the trim flag to `true` and if the boolean `false` to `false`. If the value provided is a string, sets the trim flag to the provided value, which must be one of "true", "false", "start" or "end".

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.url
documentation: For a Table: This link property gives the single URL of the CSV file that the table is held in, relative to the location of the metadata document.  For a Transformation: A link property giving the single URL of the file that the script or template is held in, relative to the location of the metadata document.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.valueUrl
documentation: An URI template property that is used to map the values of cells into URLs.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.virtual
documentation: A boolean atomic property taking a single value which indicates whether the column is a virtual column not present in the original source

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Cell
documentation: A Cell represents a cell at the intersection of a Row and a Column within a Table.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Datatype
documentation: Describes facets of a datatype.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Dialect
documentation: A Dialect Description provides hints to parsers about how to parse a linked file.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Direction
documentation: The class of table/text directions.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.ForeignKey
documentation: Describes relationships between Columns in one or more Tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.NumericFormat
documentation: If the datatype is a numeric type, the format property indicates the expected format for that number. Its value must be either a single string or an object with one or more properties.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Row
documentation: A Row represents a horizontal arrangement of cells within a Table.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Schema
documentation: A Schema is a definition of a tabular format that may be common to multiple tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Table
documentation: An annotated table is a table that is annotated with additional metadata.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.TableGroup
documentation: A Group of Tables comprises a set of Annotated Tables and a set of annotations that relate to those Tables.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.TableReference
documentation: An object property that identifies a referenced table and a set of referenced columns within that table.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.Transformation
documentation: A Transformation Definition is a definition of how tabular data can be transformed into another format.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.JSON
documentation: A literal containing JSON.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.auto
documentation: Indicates whether the tables in the group should be displayed based on the first character in the table that has a specific direction.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.inherit
documentation: For `textDirection`, indicates that the direction is inherited from the `tableDirection` annotation of the `table`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.ltr
documentation: Indicates whether the tables in the group should be displayed with the first column on the right.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._CSVW.CSVW.rtl
documentation: Indicates whether the tables in the group should be displayed with the first column on the left.

This document describes the RDFS vocabulary description used in the Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data
[[tabular-metadata]] along with the default JSON-LD Context.
