class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ask
documentation: The SPARQL ASK query to execute.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.closed
documentation: If set to true then the shape is closed.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.condition
documentation: The shapes that the focus nodes need to conform to before a rule is executed on them.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.conforms
documentation: True if the validation did not produce any validation results, and false otherwise.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.construct
documentation: The SPARQL CONSTRUCT query to execute.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.datatype
documentation: Specifies an RDF datatype that all value nodes must have.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.deactivated
documentation: If set to true then all nodes conform to this.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.declare
documentation: Links a resource with its namespace prefix declarations.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.defaultValue
documentation: A default value for a property, for example for user interface tools to pre-populate input fields.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.description
documentation: Human-readable descriptions for the property in the context of the surrounding shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.detail
documentation: Links a result with other results that provide more details, for example to describe violations against nested shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.disjoint
documentation: Specifies a property where the set of values must be disjoint with the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.entailment
documentation: An entailment regime that indicates what kind of inferencing is required by a shapes graph.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.equals
documentation: Specifies a property that must have the same values as the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.expression
documentation: The node expression that must return true for the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.focusNode
documentation: The focus node that was validated when the result was produced.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.group
documentation: Can be used to link to a property group to indicate that a property shape belongs to a group of related property shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.hasValue
documentation: Specifies a value that must be among the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ignoredProperties
documentation: An optional RDF list of properties that are also permitted in addition to those explicitly enumerated via sh:property/sh:path.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.intersection
documentation: A list of node expressions that shall be intersected.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.inversePath
documentation: The (single) value of this property represents an inverse path (object to subject).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.js
documentation: Constraints expressed in JavaScript.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.jsFunctionName
documentation: The name of the JavaScript function to execute.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.jsLibrary
documentation: Declares which JavaScript libraries are needed to execute this.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.jsLibraryURL
documentation: Declares the URLs of a JavaScript library. This should be the absolute URL of a JavaScript file. Implementations may redirect those to local files.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.labelTemplate
documentation: Outlines how human-readable labels of instances of the associated Parameterizable shall be produced. The values can contain {?paramName} as placeholders for the actual values of the given parameter.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.lessThan
documentation: Specifies a property that must have smaller values than the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.lessThanOrEquals
documentation: Specifies a property that must have smaller or equal values than the value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.maxExclusive
documentation: Specifies the maximum exclusive value of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.maxInclusive
documentation: Specifies the maximum inclusive value of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.maxLength
documentation: Specifies the maximum string length of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.message
documentation: A human-readable message (possibly with placeholders for variables) explaining the cause of the result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.minExclusive
documentation: Specifies the minimum exclusive value of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.minInclusive
documentation: Specifies the minimum inclusive value of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.minLength
documentation: Specifies the minimum string length of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.name
documentation: Human-readable labels for the property in the context of the surrounding shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.namespace
documentation: The namespace associated with a prefix in a prefix declaration.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.node
documentation: Specifies the node shape that all value nodes must conform to.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.nodeKind
documentation: Specifies the node kind (e.g. IRI or literal) each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.nodeValidator
documentation: The validator(s) used to evaluate a constraint in the context of a node shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.nodes
documentation: The node expression producing the input nodes of a filter shape expression.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.oneOrMorePath
documentation: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched one or more times.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.optional
documentation: Indicates whether a parameter is optional.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.order
documentation: Specifies the relative order of this compared to its siblings. For example use 0 for the first, 1 for the second.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.parameter
documentation: The parameters of a function or constraint component.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.path
documentation: Specifies the property path of a property shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.pattern
documentation: Specifies a regular expression pattern that the string representations of the value nodes must match.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.predicate
documentation: An expression producing the properties that shall be inferred as predicates.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.prefix
documentation: The prefix of a prefix declaration.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.prefixes
documentation: The prefixes that shall be applied before parsing the associated SPARQL query.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.property
documentation: Links a shape to its property shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.propertyValidator
documentation: The validator(s) used to evaluate a constraint in the context of a property shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.qualifiedValueShapesDisjoint
documentation: Can be used to mark the qualified value shape to be disjoint with its sibling shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.result
documentation: The validation results contained in a validation report.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.resultAnnotation
documentation: Links a SPARQL validator with zero or more sh:ResultAnnotation instances, defining how to derive additional result properties based on the variables of the SELECT query.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.resultMessage
documentation: Human-readable messages explaining the cause of the result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.resultPath
documentation: The path of a validation result, based on the path of the validated property shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.resultSeverity
documentation: The severity of the result, e.g. warning.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.rule
documentation: The rules linked to a shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.select
documentation: The SPARQL SELECT query to execute.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.severity
documentation: Defines the severity that validation results produced by a shape must have. Defaults to sh:Violation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.shapesGraphWellFormed
documentation: If true then the validation engine was certain that the shapes graph has passed all SHACL syntax requirements during the validation process.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.sourceShape
documentation: The shape that is was validated when the result was produced.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.sparql
documentation: Links a shape with SPARQL constraints.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.subject
documentation: An expression producing the resources that shall be inferred as subjects.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.suggestedShapesGraph
documentation: Suggested shapes graphs for this ontology. The values of this property may be used in the absence of specific sh:shapesGraph statements.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.target
documentation: Links a shape to a target specified by an extension language, for example instances of sh:SPARQLTarget.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.targetClass
documentation: Links a shape to a class, indicating that all instances of the class must conform to the shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.targetNode
documentation: Links a shape to individual nodes, indicating that these nodes must conform to the shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.targetObjectsOf
documentation: Links a shape to a property, indicating that all all objects of triples that have the given property as their predicate must conform to the shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.targetSubjectsOf
documentation: Links a shape to a property, indicating that all subjects of triples that have the given property as their predicate must conform to the shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.union
documentation: A list of node expressions that shall be used together.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.uniqueLang
documentation: Specifies whether all node values must have a unique (or no) language tag.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.update
documentation: The SPARQL UPDATE to execute.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.validator
documentation: The validator(s) used to evaluate constraints of either node or property shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.value
documentation: An RDF node that has caused the result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.xone
documentation: Specifies a list of shapes so that the value nodes must conform to exactly one of the shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.zeroOrMorePath
documentation: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched zero or more times.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.zeroOrOnePath
documentation: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched zero or one times.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.AbstractResult
documentation: The base class of validation results, typically not instantiated directly.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ConstraintComponent
documentation: The class of constraint components.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Function
documentation: The class of SHACL functions.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSConstraint
documentation: The class of constraints backed by a JavaScript function.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSExecutable
documentation: Abstract base class of resources that declare an executable JavaScript.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSFunction
documentation: The class of SHACL functions that execute a JavaScript function when called.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSLibrary
documentation: Represents a JavaScript library, typically identified by one or more URLs of files to include.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSRule
documentation: The class of SHACL rules expressed using JavaScript.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSTarget
documentation: The class of targets that are based on JavaScript functions.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSTargetType
documentation: The (meta) class for parameterizable targets that are based on JavaScript functions.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSValidator
documentation: A SHACL validator based on JavaScript. This can be used to declare SHACL constraint components that perform JavaScript-based validation when used.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.NodeKind
documentation: The class of all node kinds, including sh:BlankNode, sh:IRI, sh:Literal or the combinations of these: sh:BlankNodeOrIRI, sh:BlankNodeOrLiteral, sh:IRIOrLiteral.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.NodeShape
documentation: A node shape is a shape that specifies constraint that need to be met with respect to focus nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Parameter
documentation: The class of parameter declarations, consisting of a path predicate and (possibly) information about allowed value type, cardinality and other characteristics.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Parameterizable
documentation: Superclass of components that can take parameters, especially functions and constraint components.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.PrefixDeclaration
documentation: The class of prefix declarations, consisting of pairs of a prefix with a namespace.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.PropertyGroup
documentation: Instances of this class represent groups of property shapes that belong together.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.PropertyShape
documentation: A property shape is a shape that specifies constraints on the values of a focus node for a given property or path.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ResultAnnotation
documentation: A class of result annotations, which define the rules to derive the values of a given annotation property as extra values for a validation result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Rule
documentation: The class of SHACL rules. Never instantiated directly.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLAskValidator
documentation: The class of validators based on SPARQL ASK queries. The queries are evaluated for each value node and are supposed to return true if the given node conforms.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLConstraint
documentation: The class of constraints based on SPARQL SELECT queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLExecutable
documentation: The class of resources that encapsulate a SPARQL query.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLRule
documentation: The class of SHACL rules based on SPARQL CONSTRUCT queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLSelectValidator
documentation: The class of validators based on SPARQL SELECT queries. The queries are evaluated for each focus node and are supposed to produce bindings for all focus nodes that do not conform.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLTarget
documentation: The class of targets that are based on SPARQL queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLTargetType
documentation: The (meta) class for parameterizable targets that are based on SPARQL queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Severity
documentation: The class of validation result severity levels, including violation and warning levels.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Shape
documentation: A shape is a collection of constraints that may be targeted for certain nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Target
documentation: The base class of targets such as those based on SPARQL queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.TargetType
documentation: The (meta) class for parameterizable targets.	Instances of this are instantiated as values of the sh:target property.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.TripleRule
documentation: A rule based on triple (subject, predicate, object) pattern.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ValidationReport
documentation: The class of SHACL validation reports.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ValidationResult
documentation: The class of validation results.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Validator
documentation: The class of validators, which provide instructions on how to process a constraint definition. This class serves as base class for the SPARQL-based validators and other possible implementations.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.this
documentation: A node expression that represents the current focus node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.AndConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to test whether a value node conforms to all members of a provided list of shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ClassConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that each value node is an instance of a given type.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ClosedConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to indicate that focus nodes must only have values for those properties that have been explicitly enumerated via sh:property/sh:path.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.DatatypeConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the datatype of all value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.DisjointConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that the set of value nodes is disjoint with the the set of nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.EqualsConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that the set of value nodes is equal to the set of nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.ExpressionConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that a given node expression produces true for all value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.HasValueConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that one of the value nodes is a given RDF node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.InConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to exclusively enumerate the permitted value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.JSConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component with the parameter sh:js linking to a sh:JSConstraint containing a sh:script.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.LanguageInConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to enumerate language tags that all value nodes must have.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.LessThanConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that each value node is smaller than all the nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.LessThanOrEqualsConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that every value node is smaller than all the nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MaxCountConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the maximum number of value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MaxExclusiveConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a maximum exclusive value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MaxInclusiveConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a maximum inclusive value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MaxLengthConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the maximum string length of value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MinCountConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the minimum number of value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MinExclusiveConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a minimum exclusive value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MinInclusiveConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a minimum inclusive value.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.MinLengthConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the minimum string length of value nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.NodeConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that all value nodes conform to the given node shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.NodeKindConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the RDF node kind of each value node.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.NotConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that value nodes do not conform to a given shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.OrConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the value nodes so that they conform to at least one out of several provided shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.PatternConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that every value node matches a given regular expression.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.PropertyConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that all value nodes conform to the given property shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.QualifiedMaxCountConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that a specified maximum number of value nodes conforms to a given shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.QualifiedMinCountConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to verify that a specified minimum number of value nodes conforms to a given shape.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.SPARQLConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to define constraints based on SPARQL queries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.UniqueLangConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to specify that no pair of value nodes may use the same language tag.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.XoneConstraintComponent
documentation: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the value nodes so that they conform to exactly one out of several provided shapes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.BlankNode
documentation: The node kind of all blank nodes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.BlankNodeOrIRI
documentation: The node kind of all blank nodes or IRIs.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.BlankNodeOrLiteral
documentation: The node kind of all blank nodes or literals.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.IRI
documentation: The node kind of all IRIs.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.IRIOrLiteral
documentation: The node kind of all IRIs or literals.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Literal
documentation: The node kind of all literals.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Info
documentation: The severity for an informational validation result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Violation
documentation: The severity for a violation validation result.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SH.SH.Warning
documentation: The severity for a warning validation result.

W3C Shapes Constraint Language (SHACL) Vocabulary

This vocabulary defines terms used in SHACL, the W3C Shapes Constraint Language.

Generated from: https://www.w3.org/ns/shacl.ttl
Date: 2020-05-26 14:20:08.041103

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.attribute
documentation: An alternative to qb:componentProperty which makes explicit that the component is a attribute

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.codeList
documentation: gives the code list associated with a CodedProperty

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.component
documentation: indicates a component specification which is included in the structure of the dataset

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.componentAttachment
documentation: Indicates the level at which the component property should be attached, this might an qb:DataSet, qb:Slice or qb:Observation, or a qb:MeasureProperty.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.componentProperty
documentation: indicates a ComponentProperty (i.e. attribute/dimension) expected on a DataSet, or a dimension fixed in a SliceKey

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.componentRequired
documentation: Indicates whether a component property is required (true) or optional (false) in the context of a DSD. Only applicable     to components correspond to an attribute. Defaults to false (optional).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.concept
documentation: gives the concept which is being measured or indicated by a ComponentProperty

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.dataSet
documentation: indicates the data set of which this observation is a part

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.dimension
documentation: An alternative to qb:componentProperty which makes explicit that the component is a dimension

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.hierarchyRoot
documentation: Specifies a root of the hierarchy. A hierarchy may have multiple roots but must have at least one.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.measure
documentation: An alternative to qb:componentProperty which makes explicit that the component is a measure

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.measureDimension
documentation: An alternative to qb:componentProperty which makes explicit that the component is a measure dimension

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.measureType
documentation: Generic measure dimension, the value of this dimension indicates which measure (from the set of measures in the DSD) is being given by the obsValue (or other primary measure)

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.observationGroup
documentation: Indicates a group of observations. The domain of this property is left open so that a group may be attached to different resources and need not be restricted to a single DataSet

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.order
documentation: indicates a priority order for the components of sets with this structure, used to guide presentations - lower order numbers come before higher numbers, un-numbered components come last

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.parentChildProperty
documentation: Specifies a property which relates a parent concept in the hierarchy to a child concept.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.slice
documentation: Indicates a subset of a DataSet defined by fixing a subset of the dimensional values

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.sliceKey
documentation: indicates a slice key which is used for slices in this dataset

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.sliceStructure
documentation: indicates the sub-key corresponding to this slice

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.structure
documentation: indicates the structure to which this data set conforms

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.Attachable
documentation: Abstract superclass for everything that can have attributes and dimensions

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.AttributeProperty
documentation: The class of components which represent attributes of observations in the cube, e.g. unit of measurement

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.CodedProperty
documentation: Superclass of all coded ComponentProperties

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.ComponentProperty
documentation: Abstract super-property of all properties representing dimensions, attributes or measures

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.ComponentSet
documentation: Abstract class of things which reference one or more ComponentProperties

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.ComponentSpecification
documentation: Used to define properties of a component (attribute, dimension etc) which are specific to its usage in a DSD.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.DataSet
documentation: Represents a collection of observations, possibly organized into various slices, conforming to some common dimensional structure.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.DataStructureDefinition
documentation: Defines the structure of a DataSet or slice

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.HierarchicalCodeList
documentation: Represents a generalized hierarchy of concepts which can be used for coding. The hierarchy is defined by one or more roots together with a property which relates concepts in the hierarchy to their child concept .  The same concepts may be members of multiple hierarchies provided that different qb:parentChildProperty values are used for each hierarchy.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.MeasureProperty
documentation: The class of components which represent the measured value of the phenomenon being observed

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.Observation
documentation: A single observation in the cube, may have one or more associated measured values

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._QB.QB.ObservationGroup
documentation: A, possibly arbitrary, group of observations.

In order to avoid the possibility of "compound errors" when combining mappings across more than two concept schemes, skos:closeMatch is not declared to be a transitive property.

An RDF vocabulary for describing the basic structure and content of concept schemes such as thesauri,

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._DCAT.DCAT.service
documentation: A site or endpoint that is listed in the catalog.

The data catalog vocabulary

DCAT is an RDF vocabulary designed to facilitate interoperability between data catalogs published on the Web.
By using DCAT to describe datasets in data catalogs, publishers increase discoverability and enable
applications easily to consume metadata from multiple catalogs. It further enables decentralized publishing of
catalogs and facilitates federated dataset search across sites. Aggregated DCAT metadata can serve as a
manifest file to facilitate digital preservation. DCAT is defined at http://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat/. Any
variance between that normative document and this schema is an error in this schema.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._PROF.PROF.Profile
documentation: A named set of constraints on one or more identified base specifications or other profiles, including the identification of any implementing subclasses of datatypes, semantic interpretations, vocabularies, options and parameters of those base specifications necessary to accomplish a particular function.  This definition includes what are often called "application profiles", "metadata application profiles", or "metadata profiles".

documentation: The URL of a downloadable file with particulars such as its format and role indicated by a Resource Descriptor

documentation: A resource which describes the nature of an artifact and the role it plays in relation to a profile

documentation: The function of the described artifactresource in the expression of the Profile, such as a specification, guidance documentation, SHACL file etc.

documentation: This property indicates a Resource Descriptor described by this Profile’s base specification that is to be considered a Resource Descriptor for this Profile also

documentation: A Profile is a profile of a dct:Standard (or a Base Specification or another Profile)

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._PROF.PROF.isTransitiveProfileOf
documentation: A base specification an Profile conforms to

Profiles Vocabulary

This vocabulary is for describing relationships between standards/specifications, profiles of them and
supporting artifacts such as validating resources.  This model starts with
[http://dublincore.org/2012/06/14/dcterms#Standard](dct:Standard) entities which can either be Base
Specifications (a standard not profiling any other Standard) or Profiles (Standards which do profile others).
Base Specifications or Profiles can have Resource Descriptors associated with them that defines implementing
rules for the it. Resource Descriptors must indicate the role they play (to guide, to validate etc.) and the
formalism they adhere to (dct:format) to allow for content negotiation. A vocabulary of Resource Roles are
provided alongside this vocabulary but that list is extensible.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._OWL.OWL.real
documentation: The value space is the set of all real numbers. Does not directly provide any lexical forms.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.Feature
documentation: This class represents the top-level feature type. This class is        equivalent to GFI_Feature defined in ISO 19156:2011, and it is        superclass of all feature types.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.FeatureCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Features.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.Geometry
documentation: The class represents the top-level geometry type. This class is        equivalent to the UML class GM_Object defined in ISO 19107, and        it is superclass of all geometry types.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.GeometryCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Geometries.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.SpatialObject
documentation: The class spatial-object represents everything that can have        a spatial representation. It is superclass of feature and geometry.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.SpatialObjectCollection
documentation: A collection of individual Spatial Objects.  This is the superclass of Feature Collection and Geometry Collection.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.asGML

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.asWKT

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.asDGGS

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.coordinateDimension
documentation: The number of measurements or axes needed to describe the position of this       geometry in a coordinate system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.dimension
documentation: The topological dimension of this geometric object, which        must be less than or equal to the coordinate dimension.        In non-homogeneous collections, this will return the largest        topological dimension of the contained objects.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasMetricArea
documentation: The area of a Spatial Object in square meters.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasMetricLength
documentation: The length of a Spatial Object in meters.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasMetricSpatialAccuracy

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasMetricSize
documentation: Subproperties of this property are used to indicate the size of a Spatial Object as a measurement or estimate of one or more dimensions of the Spatial Object's spatial presence. Units are always metric (meter, square meter or cubic meter)

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasMetricVolume
documentation: The volume of a Spatial Object in cubic meters.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.isEmpty
documentation: (true) if this geometric object is the empty Geometry. If        true, then this geometric object represents the empty point        set for the coordinate space.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.isSimple
documentation: (true) if this geometric object has no anomalous geometric        points, such as self intersection or self tangency.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.spatialDimension
documentation: The number of measurements or axes needed to describe the spatial position of        this geometry in a coordinate system.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.defaultGeometry
documentation: The default geometry to be used in spatial calculations.       It is Usually the most detailed geometry.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasCentroid

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasDefaultGeometry

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._GEO.GEO.hasGeometry

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.basedAt
documentation: Indicates the site at which a person is based. We do not restrict the possibility that a person is based at multiple sites.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.changedBy
documentation: Indicates a change event which resulted in a change to this organization. Depending on the event the organization may or may not have continued to exist after the event. Inverse of `org:originalOrganization`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.classification
documentation: Indicates a classification for this Organization within some classification scheme. Extension vocabularies may wish to specialize this property to have a range corresponding to a specific `skos:ConceptScheme`. This property is under discussion and may be revised or removed - in many cases organizations are best categorized by defining a sub-class hierarchy in an extension vocabulary.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasMember
documentation: Indicates a person who is a member of the subject Organization. Inverse of `org:memberOf`, see that property for further clarification. Provided for compatibility with `foaf:member`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasMembership
documentation: Indicates a membership relationship that the Agent plays. Inverse of `org:member`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasPost
documentation: Indicates a Post which exists within the Organization.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasPrimarySite
documentation: Indicates a primary site for the Organization, this is the default means by which an Organization can be contacted and is not necessarily the formal headquarters.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasRegisteredSite
documentation: Indicates the legally registered site for the organization, in many legal jurisdictions there is a requirement that FormalOrganizations such as Companies or Charities have such a primary designed site.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasSite
documentation: Indicates a site at which the Organization has some presence even if only indirect (e.g. virtual office or a professional service which is acting as the registered address for a company). Inverse of `org:siteOf`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasSubOrganization
documentation: Represents hierarchical containment of Organizations or Organizational Units; indicates an organization which is a sub-part or child of this organization.  Inverse of `org:subOrganizationOf`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.hasUnit
documentation: Indicates a unit which is part of this Organization, e.g. a Department within a larger FormalOrganization. Inverse of `org:unitOf`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.headOf
documentation: Indicates that a person is the leader or formal head of the Organization. This will normally mean that they are the root of the `org:reportsTo` (acyclic) graph, though an organization may have more than one head.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.heldBy
documentation: Indicates an Agent which holds a Post.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.holds
documentation: Indicates a Post held by some Agent.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.identifier
documentation: Gives an identifier, such as a company registration number, that can be used to used to uniquely identify the organization. Many different national and international identier schemes are available. The org ontology is neutral to which schemes are used. The particular identifier scheme should be indicated by the datatype of the identifier value.  Using datatypes to distinguish the notation scheme used is consistent with recommended best practice for `skos:notation` of which this property is a specialization.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.linkedTo
documentation: Indicates an arbitrary relationship between two organizations. Specializations of this can be used to, for example, denote funding or supply chain relationships.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.location
documentation: Gives a location description for a person within the organization, for example a _Mail Stop_ for internal posting purposes.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.member
documentation: Indicates the Person (or other Agent including Organization) involved in the Membership relationship. Inverse of `org:hasMembership`

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.memberDuring
documentation: Optional property to indicate the interval for which the membership is/was valid.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.memberOf
documentation: Indicates that a person is a member of the Organization with no indication of the nature of that membership or the role played. Note that the choice of property name is not meant to limit the property to only formal membership arrangements, it is also intended to cover related concepts such as affilliation or other involvement in the organization. Extensions can specialize this relationship to indicate particular roles within the organization or more nuanced relationships to the organization. Has an optional inverse, `org:hasmember`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.organization
documentation: Indicates Organization in which the Agent is a member.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.originalOrganization
documentation: Indicates one or more organizations that existed before the change event. Depending on the event they may or may not have continued to exist after the event. Inverse of `org:changedBy`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.postIn
documentation: Indicates the Organization in which the Post exists.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.purpose
documentation: Indicates the purpose of this Organization. There can be many purposes at different levels of abstraction but the nature of an organization is to have a reason for existence and this property is a means to document that reason. An Organization may have multiple purposes. It is recommended that the purpose be denoted by a controlled term or code list, ideally a `skos:Concept`. However, the range is left open to allow for other types of descriptive schemes. It is expected that specializations or application profiles of this vocabulary will constrain the range of the purpose. Alternative names: _remit_ _responsibility_ (esp. if applied to OrganizationalUnits such as Government Departments).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.remuneration
documentation: Indicates a salary or other reward associated with the role. Typically this will be denoted using an existing representation scheme such as `gr:PriceSpecification` but the range is left open to allow applications to specialize it (e.g. to remunerationInGBP).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.reportsTo
documentation: Indicates a reporting relationship as might be depicted on an organizational chart. The precise semantics of the reporting relationship will vary by organization but is intended to encompass both direct supervisory relationships (e.g. carrying objective and salary setting authority) and more general reporting or accountability relationships (e.g. so called _dotted line_ reporting).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.resultedFrom
documentation: Indicates an event which resulted in this organization. Inverse of `org:resultingOrganization`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.resultingOrganization
documentation: Indicates an organization which was created or changed as a result of the event. Inverse of `org:resultedFrom`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.role
documentation: Indicates the Role that the Agent plays in a Membership relationship with an Organization.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.roleProperty
documentation: This is a metalevel property which is used to annotate an `org:Role` instance with a sub-property of `org:memberOf` that can be used to directly indicate the role for easy of query. The intended semantics is a Membership relation involving the Role implies the existence of a direct property relationship through an inference rule of the form:  `{ [] org:member ?p; org:organization ?o; org:role [org:roleProperty ?r] } -> {?p ?r ?o}`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.siteAddress
documentation: Indicates an address for the site in a suitable encoding. Use of vCard (using the http://www.w3.org/TR/vcard-rdf/ vocabulary) is encouraged but the range is left open to allow other encodings to be used. The address may include email, telephone, and geo-location information and is not restricted to a physical address.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.siteOf
documentation: Indicates an Organization which has some presence at the given site. This is the inverse of `org:hasSite`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.subOrganizationOf
documentation: Represents hierarchical containment of Organizations or OrganizationalUnits; indicates an Organization which contains this Organization. Inverse of `org:hasSubOrganization`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.transitiveSubOrganizationOf
documentation: The transitive closure of subOrganizationOf, giving a representation of all organizations that contain this one. Note that technically this is a super property of the transitive closure so it could contain additional assertions but such usage is discouraged.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.unitOf
documentation: Indicates an Organization of which this Unit is a part, e.g. a Department within a larger FormalOrganization. This is the inverse of `org:hasUnit`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.ChangeEvent
documentation: Represents an event which resulted in a major change to an organization such as a merger or complete restructuring. It is intended for situations where the resulting organization is sufficient distinct from the original organizations that it has a distinct identity and distinct URI. Extension vocabularies should define sub-classes of this to denote particular categories of event. The instant or interval at which the event occurred should be given by `prov:startAtTime` and `prov:endedAtTime`, a description should be given by `dct:description`.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.FormalOrganization
documentation: An Organization which is recognized in the world at large, in particular in legal jurisdictions, with associated rights and responsibilities. Examples include a Corporation, Charity, Government or Church. Note that this is a super class of `gr:BusinessEntity` and it is recommended to use the GoodRelations vocabulary to denote Business classifications such as DUNS or NAICS.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Membership
documentation: Indicates the nature of an Agent's membership of an organization. Represents an n-ary relation between an Agent, an Organization and a Role. It is possible to directly indicate membership, independent of the specific Role, through use of the `org:memberOf` property.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Organization
documentation: Represents a collection of people organized together into a community or other social, commercial or political structure. The group has some common purpose or reason for existence which goes beyond the set of people belonging to it and can act as an Agent. Organizations are often decomposable into hierarchical structures.  It is recommended that SKOS lexical labels should be used to label the Organization. In particular `skos:prefLabel` for the primary (possibly legally recognized name), `skos:altLabel` for alternative names (trading names, colloquial names) and `skos:notation` to denote a code from a code list. Alternative names: _Collective_ _Body_ _Org_ _Group_

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.OrganizationalCollaboration
documentation: A collaboration between two or more Organizations such as a project. It meets the criteria for being an Organization in that it has an identity and defining purpose independent of its particular members but is neither a formally recognized legal entity nor a sub-unit within some larger organization. Might typically have a shorter lifetime than the Organizations within it, but not necessarily. All members are `org:Organization`s rather than individuals and those Organizations can play particular roles within the venture. Alternative names: _Project_ _Venture_  _Endeavour_ _Consortium_ _Endeavour_

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.OrganizationalUnit
documentation: An Organization such as a University Support Unit which is part of some larger FormalOrganization and only has full recognition within the context of that FormalOrganization, it is not a Legal Entity in its own right. Units can be large and complex containing other Units and even FormalOrganizations. Alternative names: _OU_ _Unit_ _Department_

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Post
documentation: A Post represents some position within an organization that exists independently of the person or persons filling it. Posts may be used to represent situations where a person is a member of an organization ex officio (for example the Secretary of State for Scotland is part of UK Cabinet by virtue of being Secretary of State for Scotland, not as an individual person). A post can be held by multiple people and hence can be treated as a organization in its own right.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Role
documentation: Denotes a role that a Person or other Agent can take in an organization. Instances of this class describe the abstract role; to denote a specific instance of a person playing that role in a specific organization use an instance of `org:Membership`. It is common for roles to be arranged in some taxonomic structure and we use SKOS to represent that. The normal SKOS lexical properties should be used when labelling the Role. Additional descriptive properties for the Role, such as a Salary band, may be added by extension vocabularies.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Site
documentation: An office or other premise at which the organization is located. Many organizations are spread across multiple sites and many sites will host multiple locations. In most cases a Site will be a physical location. However, we don't exclude the possibility of non-physical sites such as a virtual office with an associated post box and phone reception service. Extensions may provide subclasses to denote particular types of site.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ORG.ORG.Head
documentation: head

Core organization ontology

Vocabulary for describing organizational structures, specializable to a broad variety of types of
organization.

Generated from: http://www.w3.org/ns/org#
Date: 2020-05-26 14:20:02.908408

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.action
documentation: The operation relating to the Asset for which the Rule is being subjected.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.andSequence
documentation: The relation is satisfied when each of the Constraints are satisfied in the order specified.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.assignee
documentation: The Party is the recipient of the Rule.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.assigneeOf
documentation: Identifies an ODRL Policy for which the identified Party undertakes the assignee functional role.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.assigner
documentation: The Party is the issuer of the Rule.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.assignerOf
documentation: Identifies an ODRL Policy for which the identified Party undertakes the assigner functional role.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.attributedParty
documentation: The Party to be attributed.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.attributingParty
documentation: The Party who undertakes the attribution.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.compensatedParty
documentation: The Party is the recipient of the compensation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.compensatingParty
documentation: The Party that is the provider of the compensation.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.conflict
documentation: The conflict-resolution strategy for a Policy.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.consentedParty
documentation: The Party who obtains the consent.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.consentingParty
documentation: The Party to obtain consent from.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.consequence
documentation: Relates a Duty to another Duty, the latter being a consequence of not fulfilling the former.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.constraint
documentation: Constraint applied to a Rule

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.contractedParty
documentation: The Party who is being contracted.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.contractingParty
documentation: The Party who is offering the contract.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.dataType
documentation: The datatype of the value of the rightOperand or rightOperandReference of a Constraint.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.duty
documentation: Relates an individual Duty to a Permission.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._ODRL2.ODRL2.failure

The ODRL Vocabulary and Expression defines a set of concepts and terms (the vocabulary) and encoding mechanism
(the expression) for permissions and obligations statements describing digital content usage based on the ODRL
Information Model.

classes.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.Deployment
documentation: Describes the Deployment of one or more Systems for a particular purpose. Deployment may be done on a Platform.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.Input
documentation: Any information that is provided to a Procedure for its use.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.Output
documentation: Any information that is reported from a Procedure.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.Property
documentation: A quality of an entity. An aspect of an entity that is intrinsic to and cannot exist without the entity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.Stimulus
documentation: An event in the real world that 'triggers' the Sensor. The properties associated to the Stimulus may be different to the eventual observed ObservableProperty. It is the event, not the object, that triggers the Sensor.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.System
documentation: System is a unit of abstraction for pieces of infrastructure that implement Procedures. A System may have components, its subsystems, which are other systems.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.deployedOnPlatform
documentation: Relation between a Deployment and the Platform on which the Systems are deployed.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.deployedSystem
documentation: Relation between a Deployment and a deployed System.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.detects
documentation: A relation from a Sensor to the Stimulus that the Sensor detects. The Stimulus itself will be serving as a proxy for some ObservableProperty.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.forProperty
documentation: A relation between some aspect of an entity and a Property.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.hasDeployment
documentation: Relation between a System and a Deployment, recording that the System is deployed in that Deployment.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.hasInput
documentation: Relation between a Procedure and an Input to it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.hasOutput
documentation: Relation between a Procedure and an Output of it.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.hasProperty
documentation: Relation between an entity and a Property of that entity.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.hasSubSystem
documentation: Relation between a System and its component parts.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.implementedBy
documentation: Relation between a Procedure (an algorithm, procedure or method) and an entity that implements that Procedure in some executable way.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.implements
documentation: Relation between an entity that implements a Procedure in some executable way and the Procedure (an algorithm, procedure or method).

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.inDeployment
documentation: Relation between a Platform and a Deployment, meaning that the deployedSystems of the Deployment are hosted on the Platform.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.isPropertyOf
documentation: Relation between a Property and the entity it belongs to.

class_variables.name: rdflib.namespace._SSN.SSN.isProxyFor
documentation: A relation from a Stimulus to the Property that the Stimulus is serving as a proxy for.

Semantic Sensor Network Ontology

This ontology describes sensors, actuators and observations, and related concepts. It does not describe domain
concepts, time, locations, etc. these are intended to be included from other ontologies via OWL imports.
